commit.message:
Switch to 8 columns to match reference game layout

Reference screenshots (8×11 grid, 75px cells) confirmed 8 columns
is the canonical layout. With 8 cols the 1-9 sequence wraps with
a natural offset — row 1 starts with 9 1 2 3... instead of
repeating the same values as row 0.

This creates real strategic choices at game start: the 3 initial
valid pairs (1+9 vertical col0, 9+1 flat row-wrap, 1+9 vertical
col1) share cells, so order matters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(install): fix bootstrap failure on machines without git pre-installed

Root cause: install.ps1 tried git clone before installing git. On a fresh
machine (no git), the clone silently failed, SCRIPT_DIR stayed as \C:\Users\MARCUS~1\AppData\Local\Temp,
REPO_ROOT resolved to the wrong parent dir, and all file copy operations
failed. The install summary falsely showed all PASS.

Fixes:
- Move source clone to a Get-SourceFiles function, called after prereqs
- After git installs (Step 1), retry source clone if it failed at startup
- Fix REPO_ROOT: use Split-Path after SCRIPT_DIR is finalized, not Resolve-Path
- Fix result tracking: persona, skills, canvas, shortcut, tests, workiq all
  now set FAIL/WARN when the file operation actually fails instead of PASS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(nexkit-file-watcher): add file watcher service for .nexkit directory protection

Implements NexkitFileWatcherService that monitors the .nexkit/ directory for
external file changes. When a user manually modifies a managed file, the
service rolls back the change and prompts for confirmation. Changes made
internally by Nexkit are suppressed to avoid false positives.

Features:
- FileSystemWatcher for .nexkit/**/* with content cache for rollback
- Per-path suppression (suppressPath) for individual file operations
- Bulk suppression (beginBulkOperation/endBulkOperation) for batch operations
- Rollback + user confirmation on file change/delete
- Warning on manual file creation in managed directory
- Singleton pattern for easy access from any service

Integration:
- TemplateFileOperations: suppress before install/uninstall
- BackupService: bulk suppress during delete/restore
- NexkitFileMigrationService: bulk suppress during migration
- ServiceContainer: registered and disposed
- extension.ts: watcher started on activation

Co-authored-by: decarufe <1083536+decarufe@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(nexkit-file-watcher): add unit tests for NexkitFileWatcherService

Add tests for singleton pattern, suppression methods, bulk operations,
disposal, and graceful handling of missing workspace folders.
Also update serviceContainer.test.ts to verify the new service.

Co-authored-by: decarufe <1083536+decarufe@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(nexkit-file-watcher): address code review feedback

- Use beginBulkOperation/endBulkOperation for directory template installs

commit.message:
chore: commit CodeQL build script and document fast-path workflow

Add .github/scripts/codeql-build.sh as a committed, reusable build script
for local CodeQL analysis. Replace ad-hoc script creation pattern that
wasted 30+ minutes per session with a 2-minute fast-path:

  gh codeql database create /tmp/codeql-db-analyzer --overwrite \
    --language=cpp --command='.github/scripts/codeql-build.sh' \
    --source-root=$(pwd)
  gh codeql database analyze /tmp/codeql-db-analyzer ...

Anti-patterns documented: never re-install gh-codeql, never re-download
query packs, never recreate build scripts ad-hoc, never iterate on
build flags. All prerequisites are pre-installed and persistent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: relaunch.sh — prefer ~/.dotnet SDK and skip Xcode version check (#324)

## Problem

`relaunch.sh` fails on machines where:

1. **.NET 10 SDK is installed via `dotnet-install.sh`** — installs to
`~/.dotnet/`, but the system `dotnet` at `/usr/local/share/dotnet/` is
an older version (e.g. 7.0). The script's bare `dotnet build` picks the
wrong one.

2. **Xcode is a minor version ahead** of what the .NET MacCatalyst SDK
expects (e.g. Xcode 26.3 vs expected 26.2), causing the build to fail
with a version mismatch error.

## Fix

- **PATH**: Prepend `~/.dotnet` to PATH if `~/.dotnet/dotnet` exists.
Conditional — no-op on machines with a single system-wide install.
- **Xcode**: Pass `-p:ValidateXcodeVersion=false` to `dotnet build`.
Apple ships Xcode updates faster than .NET SDK updates, so this mismatch
is common.

commit.message:
docs: fix curl examples to use GET and document HEAD pass-through behavior (#1699)

Manael skips conversion for non-`GET` requests (no response body to
read), so `curl -sI` (which sends `HEAD` by default) never triggers
conversion — confusing users who expect to see `Content-Type:
image/webp`.

## Changes

- **`curl` examples** — replace `curl -sI` with `curl -sI -X GET` in all
three docs files so examples actually exercise the conversion path
- **HEAD pass-through note** — add a callout in `README.md`,
`en/docs/usage/_index.md`, and `ja/docs/usage/_index.md` explicitly
stating that only `GET` requests are converted; `HEAD` and other methods
are proxied unchanged

```console
# Before (sends HEAD → no conversion)
curl -sI -H "Accept: image/webp" http://localhost:8080/image.jpg

# After (forces GET → conversion fires)
curl -sI -X GET -H "Accept: image/webp" http://localhost:8080/image.jpg
```

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

>

> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Docs: Update curl commands to use GET and document
pass-through behavior for HEAD requests</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, the documentation and README show examples using `curl -sI`
to test image conversion. However, `curl -I` sends a `HEAD` request by
default. Manael relies on reading the response body to determine
metadata (like file size and APNG detection) before performing the
conversion. Because `HEAD` requests do not return a body, Manael
intentionally skips conversion for `HEAD` requests (and any non-`GET`
methods) and passes the upstream response through unchanged.
> This can confuse users testing the proxy, as they won't see the
expected `Content-Type` changes.
> 
> ### Proposal
> 1. Update all `curl -sI` examples in the documentation to `curl -sI -X
GET`. This forces a `GET` request (triggering the conversion) while
still allowing curl to abort after reading the headers, keeping the
terminal output clean.
> 2. Add a clear note in the documentation explicitly stating that
Manael only processes `GET` requests, and that `HEAD` (and other HTTP
methods) are passed through to the upstream server without image
conversion.
>

brief note about `GET` requests.
> - [ ] **Update `website/content/en/docs/usage/_index.md`**:
>   - Replace all instances of `curl -sI` with `curl -sI -X GET`.
>   - Add a note explaining that `HEAD` requests are not converted.
> - [ ] **Update `website/content/ja/docs/usage/_index.md`**:
>   - Replace all instances of `curl -sI` with `curl -sI -X GET`.
> - Add the corresponding note in Japanese explaining that `HEAD`
requests are not converted.
>

requests (and other HTTP methods) are passed through to the upstream
server unchanged. When testing from the command line, use `curl -sI -X
GET` to fetch the headers of a converted image.

Detect self-contained projects in DotNetComponentDetector (#1689)

* Detect self-contained projects in DotNetComponentDetector

Report whether a .NET project target is self-contained by appending -selfcontained to the projectType (e.g. application-selfcontained).

Uses a heuristic based on the assets file: for each target framework, if any PackageDownload name starts with a FrameworkReference name followed by .Runtime, the target is considered self-contained. This covers both SelfContained=true and PublishAot=true scenarios, as both result in runtime package downloads in the assets file.

This relationship is not a guarantee.  The actual relationship is defined by the SDK's KnownFramework items, but we can't read those from build assets.  This convention has been followed for all in-support framework versions and should be acceptable.

Replace NetworkSet::Receive/Send system sets in PreUpdate with dedicated
Bevy schedules inserted into MainScheduleOrder:

  PreUpdate > NetworkReceive > StateTransition > ... > PostUpdate > NetworkSend

NetworkReceive drains inbound async events and runs module reaction
systems (on-connect sends, orchestration, stream handlers). NetworkSend
runs outbound broadcasts after all gameplay and PostUpdate mutations.

Within NetworkReceive, NetworkSet::Drain and NetworkSet::Commands provide
intra-schedule ordering; module systems run between them by default.

Broadcast systems (broadcast_state, broadcast_gas_grid,
broadcast_item_event) moved from Update to NetworkSend so clients always
receive fully-committed world state.

commit.message:
Scale difficulty via classic sequence repetition (option 2)

Replace snake-generated random boards with four classic-sequence
variants of increasing length. The 1-9 repeating sequence creates
genuine forced dependencies — clearing one number unlocks others
rather than every pair being a direct neighbor.

Variants (all at 6 columns wide):
  Intro    1-9 x 2  = 18 cells,  3 initial valid pairs
  Standard 1-9 x 4  = 36 cells,  7 initial valid pairs
  Hard     1-9 x 6  = 54 cells, 11 initial valid pairs
  Expert   1-9 x 8  = 72 cells, 15 initial valid pairs

Removed: generateByConstruction, buildWorker, generateRandom,
Web Worker, activeWorker, genStatus/genAttempt UI elements.
Board creation is now instant and synchronous.

Fixes #4621. Replace TimeField/DateField with new TimeEditor/DateEditor based on TextValidateField (#4622)

* Initial plan

* Add TimeTextProvider and TimeEditor implementation

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive test coverage for TryManualParse, cursor navigation, and edge cases; fix trailing whitespace

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* TextAlignment = Alignment.End is broken

Improve char-level validation in text input controls

- Add VerifyChar to ITextValidateProvider and implement in NetMaskedTextProvider, TextRegexProvider, and TimeTextProvider
- TextValidateField now blocks invalid chars before insertion and updates cursor more responsively
- Enhance cursor and navigation logic, allowing cursor past last char and better handling for right-aligned/fixed fields
- Time input fields now restrict input to valid digits/AM/PM and have improved docs
- UI labels for masked/regex fields now reflect current mask/pattern
- Minor code cleanups and spelling exception for "TimeEditor"

* Fix _isPm synchronization in TimeValue setter after f00b6db refactoring

Co-authored-by: tig <585482+tig@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix TextValidateField test failures and TimeEditor EnableForDesign crash

Revert breaking changes to TextValidateField that were introduced for
TimeEditor but broke existing TVF tests: Width+1, EndKeyHandler+1,
CursorRight past end, mouse click past CursorEnd, OnKeyDownNotHandled
rewrite, BackspaceKeyHandler, and GetMargins End alignment.

Add explicit IDesignable implementation to TimeEditor to prevent
InvalidCastException when AllViewsTests calls EnableForDesign.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TimeEditor cursor/insertion by normalizing time format patterns

Single-digit format specifiers (h, H, m, s) caused variable-width
fields where cursor positions shifted based on the current time value.
Typing "12" at position 0 of a single-digit hour would place "1" in
hours and "2" in minutes because CursorRight skipped the separator
at position 1.

Normalize all format specifiers to 2-digit versions (hh, HH, mm, ss)
so field positions are always consistent. Remove the leading space
hack from DisplayText since padding is no longer needed.

Add 9 unit tests covering cursor navigation, insertion at all digit
positions, separator skipping, and DriverAssert rendering verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve TimeEditor width and TextValidateField cursor logic

- TimeEditor now sets width to fit full DisplayText (incl. AM/PM).
- TextValidateField allows cursor one past last editable char.
- Fixed width calculation for fixed fields (DisplayText.Length + 1).
- Updated tests for new width and cursor behavior.
- Added labels in TimeAndDate demo to show time patterns.
- Added tests to verify full AM/PM visibility and width logic.
- Improves usability and prevents clipping of time strings.

* Improve cursor handling at end of masked text fields

Enhances navigation and editing in masked fields (TextValidateField, TimeEditor) to prevent the cursor from moving backward when pressing right arrow from the blank cell past the last editable character. Backspace from the blank cell now deletes the last editable character. TimeEditor minimum width is increased by one to always allow a blank cell. Updates logic for right-arrow navigation in fixed providers. Adds unit tests to verify correct cursor and editing behavior at the end of input. These changes address subtle usability issues and make cursor movement more intuitive.

* Replace TimeField with new TimeEditor control

Removed the TimeField control, its tests, and documentation. Introduced TimeEditor as the new time input control, based on TextValidateField with culture-aware formatting. Updated all references, scenarios, and docs to use TimeEditor instead of TimeField. TimeField source and tests have been deleted.

* Refactor TimeEditor and providers, remove VerifyChar method

Refactored TimeEditor, TimeTextProvider, NetMaskedTextProvider, and TextRegexProvider for clarity and consistency. Removed the VerifyChar method from ITextValidateProvider and all implementations, simplifying the provider interface. Standardized event raising and improved handling of AM/PM, manual parsing, and pattern normalization in TimeTextProvider. Updated TextValidateField drawing and key handling logic for simplicity. Modernized and clarified all related tests to match the new API and style. Made various code style improvements and updated documentation throughout.

* Implement CWP and IValue<T> for TextValidateField/TimeEditor

Refactored TextValidateField and TimeEditor to fully implement the IValue<T> interface and follow the Cancellable Work Pattern (CWP) for value changes.
- TextValidateField now exposes Value, ValueChanging, and ValueChanged events, and synchronizes value changes from both programmatic and user input, with support for cancellation and reversion.
- Introduced SuppressValueEvents to prevent unwanted event recursion.
- TimeEditor now uses a private _value field, leverages CWPPropertyHelper for value changes, and raises strongly-typed TimeSpan events.
- Removed manual event wiring and last-known-value tracking in favor of the new base class pattern.
- These changes improve consistency, extensibility, and support for data binding and undo/redo scenarios.

* Replace DateField with new DateEditor based on TextValidateField

Add DateTextProvider (ITextValidateProvider for dates with culture-aware
formatting, pattern normalization, separator handling) and DateEditor
(extends TextValidateField, implements IValue<DateTime?> with CWP).
Remove legacy DateField and update all usages in DatePicker, scenarios,
docs, and indexes. Full test coverage in DateEditorTests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* code cleanup

* code cleanup

* Refactor TextChanged event handling for providers

Refactored event raising in Date, Time, Masked, and Regex text providers by introducing a private RaiseTextChanged method. OnTextChanged is now virtual and serves as a subclass hook, improving extensibility. Updated all event triggers to use RaiseTextChanged. Improved null safety in TextRegexProvider by initializing _text to an empty list. Fixed CleanSeparator logic in DateTextProvider for short separators. Enhanced TryParseDateValue to only fall back to manual parsing if needed. Updated documentation to clarify new event handling pattern.

* Fixed test bug.

- Remove Pos.Center() and center text alignment from all editors and labels
- Add inline DatePicker synced bidirectionally with default DateEditor
- Add Prompt<DatePicker> button that opens modal date picker dialog

commit.message:
feat: strict mode, WeakRef, Object.prototype.toString conformance (#449)

* Enforce strict mode semantics for undeclared vars, this binding, params, and octals

- StaGlobal/StaLookupSlot: throw ReferenceError when assigning to
  undeclared variables in strict mode instead of creating globals
- CallUndefinedReceiver0/1/2: set 'this' to undefined for strict
  callee functions (save/restore around shared global_env)
- Parser: reject duplicate simple parameter names in strict mode
  functions (checked after body parse so 'use strict' directives work)
- Parser: reject legacy octal literals (e.g. 0123) in strict mode
  (0o123 is still allowed)
- Scanner: expose source() getter for raw token text inspection
- Existing 'with' statement and 'delete variable' checks already present

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(builtins): use Weak<T> for WeakRef/FinalizationRegistry PlainObject targets

WeakRef:
- Add WeakTarget enum supporting both Rc-managed (Weak<RefCell<PropertyMap>>)
  and GC-managed (*mut HeapObject) targets
- Add weak_ref_new_plain() for PlainObject targets using Rc::downgrade()
- deref() now returns Undefined automatically when Rc target is collected
- Constructor in install_globals accepts both Object and PlainObject

FinalizationRegistry:
- Add PlainRegistration with Weak<RefCell<PropertyMap>> target tracking
- Add finalization_registry_register_plain() for PlainObject targets
- Add finalization_registry_unregister_plain() for PlainObject tokens
- Add finalization_registry_sweep_plain() to detect collected Rc targets
- cleanupSome() now sweeps plain targets before draining the queue
- register/unregister in install_globals accept both Object and PlainObject

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement spec-compliant Object.prototype.toString (§20.1.3.6)

- Add JsValue::obj_to_string_tag() that returns the correct [object X]
  string for all value types per ECMAScript spec
- Install Object.prototype.toString on the prototype object
- Update proto_lookup toString fallback to use obj_to_string_tag
- Fix NativeFunction .call()/.apply()/.bind() to pass thisArg through
  so Object.prototype.toString.call(value) works correctly
- Add __is_array__ marker to array literal PlainObjects for detection
- Add @@toStringTag = 'Date' to Date instances
- Handles: Undefined, Null, Boolean, Number, String, Symbol, BigInt,
  Array, Function, Error, Generator, RegExp, Date, Promise, and
  @@toStringTag custom tags

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
